sources.official_pricing_url: https://docs.dexscreener.com (checkout stage, tiers not separately published)
sources.authentication: None for current free endpoints (no API key in documented examples)
sources.rate_limits: 60 requests/min per documented endpoint (token-profiles, metas); third-party sources report 300 req/min for /latest/dex/search, NOT CONFIRMED from primary source for that specific endpoint
sources.commercial_use: Permitted - ToS Section 4 states API may be used for non-commercial AND commercial purposes, subject to Agreement limits
sources.public_beta_use: Permitted under same commercial terms; no separate beta carve-out found
sources.caching_rules: NOT CONFIRMED - ToS does not address caching duration explicitly
sources.storage_rules: NOT CONFIRMED - no explicit storage-duration clause in ToS
sources.redistribution_rules: Prohibited - ToS Section 2 (Resell Restrictions): no selling, marketing, licensing, or making API Services/data available to third parties
sources.derived_data_rules: Not explicitly addressed; Section 1 (Scope of Use) prohibits building a product whose primary purpose competes with DexScreener or its API
sources.attribution_requirements: NOT CONFIRMED - no attribution clause found in ToS
sources.current_endpoint: https://api.dexscreener.com/latest/dex/search?q=<query>
implementation_changes_required: Confirm exact rate limit for /latest/dex/search directly with DexScreener support before scaling request volume; documented limits cover other endpoints at 60/min
Do not expose raw API JSON responses directly to end users; pass only normalized/derived fields, since redistribution of 'any portion' of API Services to third parties is prohibited
Avoid building any feature whose primary purpose is a competing DEX-data product
Re-confirm terms before any paid tier signup, since checkout-stage terms may add conditions
open_questions: Does 'making API Services available to third parties' extend to showing derived scores to end users of a SaaS, or only to reselling raw access? NOT CONFIRMED
What exact rate limit applies to the currently used endpoint?
sources.notes: ToS explicitly allows commercial use but the no-redistribution and no-competing-product clauses are broadly worded and apply to 'any portion' of the data; legal review recommended before COMMERCIAL_ALLOWED is assigned for a paid public product.

sources.official_pricing_url: NOT CONFIRMED - no public self-serve pricing page found; Pro/Ultra/Enterprise tiers referenced but pricing not published
sources.authentication: API key/access token system exists for higher tiers; base Token Security API is described as free/open and permissionless
sources.rate_limits: NOT CONFIRMED exact numeric limit; default batch throughput up to 100 addresses per query (paid tiers only), full-corpus dumping not supported
sources.commercial_use: NOT CONFIRMED explicitly either way in the License Agreement excerpt obtained; Agreement requires display of GoPlus logo / 'Powered by GoPlus' attribution, implying commercial integration is anticipated but not unconditionally cleared
sources.public_beta_use: NOT CONFIRMED
sources.redistribution_rules: Automated bypass of rate limits via scripts/bots/crawlers/proxies is explicitly prohibited
sources.derived_data_rules: Agreement states GoPlus 'will only provide API results' and does not suggest modification to API results, and disclaims responsibility for front-end/back-end modifications made by the integrator - implies derived/modified display is the integrator's own responsibility, not a GoPlus-sanctioned activity
sources.attribution_requirements: Required - integrator 'shall display' the GoPlus logo with 'Powered by GoPlus' wording; failure can result in termination and potential legal action per the Agreement text
sources.branding_requirements: Mandatory GoPlus logo + 'Powered by GoPlus' display
sources.current_endpoint: https://api.gopluslabs.io/api/v1/token_security/{chain_id}?contract_addresses=<address>
implementation_changes_required: Add mandatory 'Powered by GoPlus' logo/attribution wherever GoPlus-derived data is shown to end users
Obtain written confirmation from GoPlus on commercial-use terms and any tier requirements before public/commercial launch
Confirm whether current no-key usage is the free/permissionless tier or requires upgrade for the planned call volume
Do not bypass rate limits via scripted bulk requests
open_questions: Is the current no-key endpoint usage within GoPlus's intended free-tier scope, or does production/commercial use require a paid tier? NOT CONFIRMED

Does the Agreement's silence on 'commercial use' mean it is permitted by default, or does it require a separate enterprise agreement? NOT CONFIRMED - the License Agreement text obtained did not include an explicit commercial-use clause
sources.notes: Full API License Agreement text could not be completely retrieved (dynamic page); analysis is based on a substantial verified excerpt including governing law, attribution mandate, and anti-bypass clause. Recommend retrieving the complete document directly before finalizing classification.

sources.official_pricing_url: NOT CONFIRMED - no published pricing page found; API key system described as 'not yet implemented'
sources.authentication: None currently required - official docs state API key system not yet implemented
sources.rate_limits: NOT CONFIRMED - no documented rate limit found
sources.commercial_use: NOT CONFIRMED explicitly; ToS contains a broad 'Third-Party Restriction' prohibiting making any portion of the API or its functionalities available to third parties, which is in tension with any user-facing commercial product
sources.public_beta_use: NOT CONFIRMED - same tension as above applies to any user-facing exposure
sources.redistribution_rules: Resale, leasing, or sublicensing of the API or its components is expressly prohibited (Resale Prohibition clause)
sources.derived_data_rules: NOT CONFIRMED - ToS does not separately address derived/normalized scores distinct from raw API access
sources.current_endpoint: https://api.honeypot.is/v2/IsHoneypot?address=<address>&chainID=<chainId>
implementation_changes_required: Seek explicit written permission from honeypot.is for the intended automated, multi-user product use case before any public or commercial launch
Keep PUBLIC_BETA and COMMERCIAL blocked until the Third-Party Restriction clause is clarified; 12R.2 authorizes only the candidate-scoped INTERNAL_BETA path
Monitor t.me/honeypotis_news as referenced in official docs for API key/terms changes
open_questions: Does showing a derived risk flag (e.g. 'high risk - possible honeypot') to end users of Crypto Edge AI constitute making the API's 'functionality' available to a third party under the Third-Party Restriction clause? NOT CONFIRMED - this is the single largest open compliance question for this source
Will an API key requirement be introduced, and will it change terms?
sources.notes: This is the most legally ambiguous source in the registry. The literal text of the Third-Party Restriction clause is broad enough to be read as prohibiting any multi-user product (since every user of such a product would be receiving the API's functionality indirectly). A conservative compliance posture treats this as POC_ONLY / internal-research-only pending direct clarification from honeypot.is.

sources.category: Market data (price, market cap, exchange, onchain)
sources.authentication: API key (header or query param) required for Demo and paid plans
sources.rate_limits: Demo plan: 100 calls/min, 10,000 calls/month (free); Pro plans add higher limits starting ~$35/mo
sources.commercial_use: Permitted on Analyst, Lite, and Pro plans, with mandatory attribution; Demo (free) plan is for prototyping and is NOT itself a commercial license per CoinGecko's own FAQ distinction between Demo and commercial plans
sources.public_beta_use: Permitted under a paid plan with attribution; NOT CONFIRMED whether the free Demo key alone is sufficient for a public-facing (even beta) product, since commercial-use language is tied to paid plans
sources.caching_rules: NOT CONFIRMED precise duration limits; general commercial terms permit incorporation into a product
sources.storage_rules: NOT CONFIRMED beyond general redistribution restriction
sources.redistribution_rules: Reselling, renting, leasing, sub-licensing, redistributing, or syndicating access to the API or any part is prohibited unless under a separate Executed Agreement
sources.derived_data_rules: Using/integrating the API into a paid product is allowed; advertising around the product is allowed
sources.attribution_requirements: Required on Analyst/Lite/Pro commercial plans: visible 'Data provided by CoinGecko' message with hyperlink to coingecko.com/en/api
sources.branding_requirements: Attribution text + hyperlink as specified above; no use of CoinGecko branding beyond that without consent
sources.current_endpoint: Not yet implemented in current codebase
implementation_changes_required: Obtain a CoinGecko API key (Demo at minimum, paid plan for any public/commercial deployment)
Add the required 'Data provided by CoinGecko' attribution with hyperlink wherever CoinGecko-sourced data appears to end users, once on a commercial plan
Confirm with CoinGecko support whether Demo-tier data may be shown to real users in a Camp BETA before launch, since the FAQ distinguishes Demo from commercial licensing
open_questions: Is the free Demo plan sufficient for a public (non-paying) BETA audience, or does any real-user exposure require the paid commercial license? NOT FULLY CONFIRMED from the pricing FAQ alone
sources.notes: GeckoTerminal onchain DEX data is provided through the same CoinGecko API account/terms per official documentation, so it is tracked under this same entry rather than as a separate source.

sources.authentication: Same CoinGecko API key system; GeckoTerminal onchain endpoints are served under the CoinGecko API umbrella
sources.rate_limits: Same tiering as main CoinGecko API (Demo 100/min, 10k/month free)
sources.commercial_use: Same CoinGecko API Terms of Service apply since GeckoTerminal data is delivered via the same API product
sources.public_beta_use: Same conditions as CoinGecko API entry above
sources.caching_rules: NOT CONFIRMED beyond general CoinGecko terms
sources.storage_rules: NOT CONFIRMED beyond general CoinGecko terms
sources.redistribution_rules: Same as CoinGecko API - no resale/redistribution without Executed Agreement
sources.attribution_requirements: Same attribution clause as CoinGecko API applies; NOT CONFIRMED whether a distinct 'Powered by GeckoTerminal' mark is separately required
sources.branding_requirements: NOT CONFIRMED for a GeckoTerminal-specific mark distinct from CoinGecko's
sources.current_endpoint: Not yet implemented in current codebase
implementation_changes_required: Treat as part of the same CoinGecko API key/account and licensing decision rather than a separate procurement
Confirm whether a GeckoTerminal-specific attribution mark is required in addition to the general CoinGecko one
open_questions: Is there a GeckoTerminal-specific attribution requirement distinct from the general CoinGecko one? NOT CONFIRMED
sources.notes: Functionally and contractually unified with the main CoinGecko API; classification mirrors that entry.

sources.category: DeFi TVL, protocol, stablecoin, and price data
sources.authentication: None required for free/open endpoints; API key only required for Pro tier ($250-$300/mo) for higher rate limits and premium endpoints
sources.rate_limits: No documented rate limit for normal traffic on the free/open API per official FAQ; Pro tier adds 1,000 req/min, 1M calls/month
sources.commercial_use: Not explicitly restricted for the free/open API in the FAQ or Terms excerpt obtained; Terms of Use impose broad liability and breach provisions (including liquidated damages up to $100,000 per violation of Clause 8, content not fully retrieved) - full Clause 8 text should be reviewed before high-volume commercial use
sources.public_beta_use: Appears permitted based on free/open API description; NOT FULLY CONFIRMED against the complete Terms of Use text (only partial text retrieved)
sources.redistribution_rules: NOT CONFIRMED from the retrieved Terms excerpt; official FAQ only requests (does not require) attribution
sources.attribution_requirements: Requested but not mandatory - official FAQ states 'Citing DefiLlama as the source is much appreciated' (not phrased as a requirement)
sources.current_endpoint: Not yet implemented in current codebase
implementation_changes_required: Add a DefiLlama attribution line as a best practice even though not strictly mandatory
Retrieve and review the full Clause 8 text of the Terms of Use (liquidated-damages clause) before scaling request volume, since only a partial excerpt was available
Monitor for rate-limit throttling and consider Pro tier if production call volume is high
open_questions: What exactly does Clause 8 of the Terms of Use prohibit, given it carries liquidated damages up to $100,000 per violation? NOT FULLY CONFIRMED - full clause text was not retrievable
sources.notes: One of the most permissive sources researched; free, keyless, and not rate-limited for normal use per the provider's own FAQ. The unresolved Clause 8 text is the only reason this is not rated APPROVED_API with full COMMERCIAL_ALLOWED confidence at HIGH+ certainty.

sources.official_pricing_url: Free - no pricing page; explicitly stated as free with no plans to change
sources.authentication: None required
sources.rate_limits: NOT CONFIRMED numerically; no documented rate limit found
sources.commercial_use: Explicitly permitted - official documentation states the API and its data may be used 'in any way you like, this includes commercial projects of any kind'
sources.public_beta_use: Explicitly permitted under the same blanket free-use statement
sources.caching_rules: Not restricted; no caching limitation found
sources.storage_rules: Not restricted; no storage limitation found
sources.redistribution_rules: None found - no redistribution restriction in official terms
sources.attribution_requirements: Appreciated, not required - official terms state attribution is 'by no means' obligatory
sources.current_endpoint: https://api.alternative.me/fng/
implementation_changes_required: No changes required for compliance; optional courtesy attribution recommended
sources.notes: Cleanest and most permissive source in the entire registry. Official terms unambiguously permit commercial use with no API key, no rate limit documentation, and only voluntary attribution.

sources.category: Ethereum (and unified EVM multichain) block explorer data
sources.authentication: API key (single key now covers 60+ EVM chains via API V2, chainid parameter)
sources.rate_limits: Free tier: documented separately per plan on etherscan.io/apis (not itemized in excerpt obtained); Pro tiers offer up to 20 calls/sec and 500,000 calls/day per a third-party source - NOT CONFIRMED as the exact current free-tier figure
sources.commercial_use: Restricted - official API Terms of Service state API Content may be viewed/cached/copied strictly for PERSONAL use, NOT commercial use, and that Content may not be used as a basis for financial or commercial gain without Etherscan's express prior written consent
sources.public_beta_use: Not confirmed as permitted - same personal-use-only restriction applies regardless of beta/production framing
sources.caching_rules: Permitted to 'cache' content but only within the personal-use restriction; commercial caching is not separately cleared
sources.storage_rules: Permitted to 'download and make copies' within personal-use restriction only
sources.redistribution_rules: May not reproduce, transmit, broadcast, publish, modify, display, distribute, sell, license, rent, lease, or create derivative forms of API Content without Etherscan's prior consent
sources.derived_data_rules: Same prohibition applies to derivative forms of API Content, per the ToS text
sources.attribution_requirements: Source attribution via link-back or 'Powered by Etherscan.io APIs' is required EXCEPT for personal/private usage (implying attribution is the minimum bar even where commercial use might otherwise be contemplated)
sources.branding_requirements: 'Powered by Etherscan.io APIs' mention or link-back
approved_endpoints: api.etherscan.io/v2/api (single multichain endpoint with chainid parameter)
sources.current_endpoint: Not yet implemented in current codebase
implementation_changes_required: Do NOT deploy any Etherscan-sourced data in a commercial or revenue-generating context (including a paid course/educational product, B2B service, or monetized app) without first obtaining Etherscan's express prior written consent
If proceeding, contact Etherscan via the documented contact channel to request explicit commercial permission before integration
If used at all pre-permission, restrict to internal/personal research only, with no end-user-facing display
If/when permission is granted, add 'Powered by Etherscan.io APIs' attribution
open_questions: Does ALLinTraders' planned use (educational content / trading analysis product) qualify for an exception, or does it require the same prior written consent as any other commercial use? NOT CONFIRMED - requires direct inquiry to Etherscan
sources.notes: This is one of the strictest sources in the registry. The 'strictly for personal use only but not for commercial use' language is unambiguous on its face and represents the highest-priority legal blocker for any source in this audit if Crypto Edge AI is or becomes a commercial product.

sources.category: BNB Smart Chain block explorer data
sources.authentication: BscScan's standalone API and API keys were deprecated; access is now exclusively through Etherscan API V2 with a chainid parameter for BNB Smart Chain
sources.rate_limits: Same as Etherscan API V2 entry
sources.commercial_use: Same personal-use-only restriction as Etherscan API applies, since BscScan is now the same legal entity/product
sources.public_beta_use: Same restriction as Etherscan API
sources.redistribution_rules: Same as Etherscan API
approved_endpoints: api.etherscan.io/v2/api?chainid=56 (BNB Smart Chain via Etherscan V2)
sources.current_endpoint: Not in current codebase; legacy api.bscscan.com endpoints are deprecated
implementation_changes_required: Do not implement or reference legacy api.bscscan.com endpoints; these are deprecated as of the V1 retirement deadline
Any BSC explorer data must be sourced via Etherscan API V2 with chainid=56, subject to the same commercial-use restriction described under the etherscan_api entry
sources.notes: BscScan is listed separately in the Priority A research list, but it is no longer a distinct API product. As of the official Etherscan API V2 migration, BscScan API keys and endpoints have been deprecated/unified into Etherscan's single multichain API and the same Terms of Service. This entry is intentionally classified BLOCKED_NO_PERMISSION as a standalone source to prevent the implementation from building against a deprecated endpoint; the live successor is tracked under etherscan_api.

sources.authentication: Pro API key required; no documented free/keyless public tier was found for programmatic access
sources.rate_limits: Tiered by paid plan: Level 2 150M CU/month at 1,000 req/min ($199/mo) up to Level 4 1.5B CU/month at 3,000 req/min ($1,099/mo)
sources.commercial_use: Ambiguous - the Pro API Terms prohibit using API Content to create a competing service, manipulating content for third-party resale, and selling/renting/leasing access for commercial purposes, but do not categorically ban all commercial integration; a product that uses (rather than resells) the data for its own end users is not explicitly cleared or barred in the excerpt obtained
sources.public_beta_use: NOT CONFIRMED - same ambiguity as commercial_use
sources.storage_rules: Must destroy API Documentation/Content/Data upon termination of access per the Terms excerpt
sources.redistribution_rules: Prohibited - 'Manipulate any API Content...for resale to a third party' and 'sell, trade, rent, loan, lease, license or provide our API Content or access to our service for commercial purposes' are both explicit prohibitions
sources.derived_data_rules: Prohibited from copying, modifying, or recreating data/content/software obtained through the API ('API Content') per the Terms excerpt
approved_endpoints: Pro API v2.0 endpoints (Account, Token, NFT, Transaction, Block, Market categories)
sources.current_endpoint: Not yet implemented in current codebase
implementation_changes_required: A paid Pro API key is required before any integration is possible; there is no free/keyless tier

Obtain explicit written clarification from Solscan on whether using (not reselling) their data inside a commercial educational/trading product is permitted under the 'commercial purposes' prohibition, since the clause is ambiguous between resale-of-access and general commercial use
Do not copy/recreate/redistribute raw API Content; only use normalized, integrated outputs
open_questions: Does the prohibition on providing 'access to our service for commercial purposes' apply only to reselling Solscan access itself, or to any commercial product that consumes Solscan data? NOT CONFIRMED - this is the central open legal question for this source
sources.notes: Functionally similar restriction pattern to Etherscan and Honeypot.is (no-resale, no-competing-product, no-redistribution clauses are common across blockchain explorer/security APIs). No free tier exists, so cost is a gating factor in addition to legal ambiguity.

sources.category: Wallet cluster / holder distribution visualization data
sources.official_terms_url: NOT CONFIRMED - no standalone API Terms of Service page was located distinct from the general developer documentation
sources.authentication: API key required, passed via X-ApiKey header; credit-based usage system
sources.rate_limits: 10,000 calls/minute rate limit applied at the IP level, in addition to a per-call credit system
sources.commercial_use: NOT CONFIRMED - no explicit commercial-use clause was located; access is gated through a 'Work with Us' B2B request process, suggesting use cases are reviewed/negotiated rather than governed by a self-serve public ToS
sources.public_beta_use: NOT CONFIRMED
sources.redistribution_rules: NOT CONFIRMED

sources.current_endpoint: Not yet implemented in current codebase

Do not proceed on the assumption that sign-up alone constitutes unrestricted commercial permission until terms are confirmed
open_questions: What are the actual contractual terms governing commercial use, redistribution, and derived-data display? NOT CONFIRMED - the publicly accessible documentation describes authentication mechanics but not a complete legal ToS

The B2B REST API (docs.bubblemaps.io/data/api) is the relevant one for Crypto Edge AI's data-licensing needs and requires direct registration.

sources.provider: CCData (CryptoCompare, now part of CoinDesk Indices)
sources.authentication: API key required for all tiers including free
sources.rate_limits: Free key: personal/non-commercial use or commercial trial; numeric limits NOT CONFIRMED from primary source in this pass
sources.commercial_use: Restricted on free tier - official Licence Agreement states even internal-use data must NOT be used in connection with any commercial product/service or 'Index'/'Financial Product' without a paid commercial licence ($80-$200+/month per third-party reporting, NOT CONFIRMED as current pricing)
sources.public_beta_use: Not permitted on the free key per the same restriction; would require a commercial subscription
sources.caching_rules: Internal use only ('you must not share it with anyone else') unless a commercial plan states otherwise
sources.storage_rules: Internal use only under free tier; commercial tiers permit broader caching/storage per CCData's commercial product description
sources.redistribution_rules: Commercial redistribution rights are only included in paid commercial tiers, not the free key
sources.derived_data_rules: NOT CONFIRMED in detail; 'Special Terms' for certain data sets may impose additional restrictions per the Agreement
sources.attribution_requirements: Required on free-tier use - 'if you're using our API with a free account, you'll have to reference us in your application' per official guide
approved_endpoints: NOT CONFIRMED - full endpoint list requires developer portal review
sources.current_endpoint: Not yet implemented in current codebase
implementation_changes_required: Do not use the free CCData/CryptoCompare key for any commercial or public-facing deployment; a paid commercial licence is required
If adopted, add required attribution per the free-tier branding guide
Confirm current pricing directly with CCData/CoinDesk Indices, since the brand has migrated under CoinDesk and pricing pages may have changed
open_questions: What is current commercial pricing now that the product operates under the CoinDesk/CCData brand rather than the original CryptoCompare branding? NOT CONFIRMED
sources.notes: Priority B - qualification only. The provider has undergone a corporate/brand transition (CryptoCompare -> CCData -> CoinDesk Indices); legacy URLs may redirect or change without notice.

sources.category: Onchain SQL analytics / blockchain query platform
sources.authentication: API key required, issued after subscribing to a plan (including the free plan)
sources.rate_limits: Free plan includes 2,500 credits and API access by default; credit consumption scales with query complexity and data exported
sources.commercial_use: NOT CONFIRMED as categorically restricted in the General Terms excerpt obtained; no explicit personal-use-only clause was found (unlike Etherscan/Solscan), but full Addenda text was not completely reviewed
sources.public_beta_use: NOT CONFIRMED in full; API Keys must be kept confidential and not sold/sublicensed to third parties
sources.redistribution_rules: User must not sell, transfer, sublicense, or disclose API Keys to third parties; broader data-redistribution terms NOT CONFIRMED
sources.derived_data_rules: User-created public content/queries may be used, forked, and copied by other Dune users per the platform's open/composable design - relevant only if Crypto Edge AI publishes dashboards on Dune itself, not for private API consumption
approved_endpoints: Query execution and results API; Sim API for wallet balances/transactions
sources.current_endpoint: Not yet implemented in current codebase
implementation_changes_required: Review the full SQL API Service Addendum and Sim API terms (only partially retrieved) before integrating
Keep API keys confidential and do not share across organizations per the explicit confidentiality clause
open_questions: Is there an explicit commercial-use restriction in the Addenda not captured in the General Terms excerpt? NOT CONFIRMED
sources.notes: Priority B - qualification only. Credit-based billing model; free tier appears usable for prototyping but full legal review of all Addenda is recommended before production use.

sources.category: Market data (price, market cap, listings, DEX)
sources.authentication: API key required for Basic (free) tier and all paid tiers; a separate rate-limited Keyless Public API exists for a curated endpoint subset
sources.rate_limits: Basic (free): 15,000 call credits/month, 50 requests/min, personal use only; commercial use begins at Startup tier ($79/mo) and above
sources.commercial_use: Restricted on Basic (free) tier to personal use only per the general Terms of Use ('strictly for your personal, non-commercial use unless you enter into a separate agreement... for your commercial use'); commercial use is explicitly approved starting at the Startup paid tier under the separate Commercial Terms of Use
sources.public_beta_use: Permitted once on a commercial-approved paid tier (Startup or above); not on the free Basic tier if end users beyond the developer will see the data
sources.caching_rules: Caching for performance/operation of the application is implied as acceptable; redistribution as a standalone data service is not
sources.storage_rules: Local caching to support the application is permitted; building a public data-redistribution mirror is not
sources.redistribution_rules: Not allowed to redistribute or resell data as a standalone service (e.g. via your own API) or as part of a data-distribution product; data must be an integrated component of a larger product for end users' personal use
sources.derived_data_rules: NOT CONFIRMED in full detail beyond the redistribution restriction above
sources.attribution_requirements: Required - visible credit such as 'Data provided by CoinMarketCap' (linked) wherever CMC data is displayed
sources.branding_requirements: Follow CMC branding guidelines if using logos; link-only attribution acceptable when in doubt
sources.current_endpoint: Not yet implemented in current codebase
implementation_changes_required: Use at minimum the Startup paid tier ($79/mo) if data will be shown to end users of a commercial product, since the free Basic tier is restricted to personal use
Add mandatory 'Data provided by CoinMarketCap' attribution with hyperlink
Do not expose data via a separate public API or as a standalone redistribution product
sources.notes: Priority B - qualification only, but terms are unusually well-documented and unambiguous compared to several Priority A sources.

sources.notes: Priority B - qualification only, and the lowest-confidence entry in the registry due to brand fragmentation. The originally-named product has rebranded, and equivalent data is also available as a feature inside DefiLlama (free, already in this registry), CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, and Messari/Artemis (all already covered above). Recommend satisfying this data need through an already-vetted source (DefiLlama is the strongest free candidate) rather than pursuing a new vendor relationship, unless Tokenomist's specific dataset is a hard requirement.
